tec.title: Terrain Effects Chart — Summary
tec.note: The full TEC with specific MP costs per terrain type appears on the Player Aid Cards (not in the rules PDF). The values below are extracted from rules text references. Consult the physical TEC for complete movement costs.

movementCovering.title: Movement Covering Terrain (MCT)
movementCovering.description: Terrain that limits an observer's ability to detect units and makes it easier to move adjacent to enemy units (7.8.0).

observationCovering.title: Observation Covering Terrain (OCT)
observationCovering.description: Terrain that provides concealment to stationary units. All MCT is also OCT.

blockingTerrain.title: LOS Blocking Terrain
blockingTerrain.description: Towns, cities, and VP are blocking terrain. Treat the entire hex as blocking, not just the artwork.

roughTerrain.title: Rough Terrain
roughTerrain.description: A LOS traced through (but not into or from) any rough terrain hex has its range reduced by one hex (minimum of one).

constrictedTerrain.title: Constricted Terrain
constrictedTerrain.description: Bluffs, steep canyons, or very rugged terrain that severely restricts movement. Dashed lines drawn from one hexside to another in the same hex indicate constricted terrain.
constrictedTerrain.movementPenalty: Mech class: entire MA to enter or exit. Leg class: +1 MP to enter and/or exit.
constrictedTerrain.avoidance: Units using Mech road movement ignore the penalty. Leg class units following any type of road also ignore it (7.6.2). Heavy/super heavy AFVs cannot use trails/RRs in constricted terrain.

movementReferences.title: Movement Cost References from Rules
rows: Leg class unit entering hex via any road (incl. trails/RR)
Leg class unit using ford to cross river
Mech road movement — clear hex (dry ground only)
Mech road movement — entering hex with 1 Mech unit already present
Night GT — Mech not on primary/secondary road
Night GT — Leg not on any road
Adjacent to enemy unit — Leg class
Adjacent to enemy unit — Mech class

groundConditions.title: Ground Condition Effects on MA
rows: No effect; Mech road movement may use clear hexes as roads (7.10.0)
Subtract 2 from MA (unless on primary/secondary road only)
Halve MA (unless on primary/secondary road only)
Halve MA (unless on primary/secondary road only)

atmosphericConditions.title: Atmospheric Condition Effects
rows: Air observation: 3 hexes; GS mission error +1 DRM; NGS subject to case E DRMs; GI resolution +1 DRM; Air supply +1 DRM
No air observation; GS mission error +2 DRM; NGS subject to case E DRMs; SI/Air supply unavailable; GI resolution subject to DRMs
No air observation; No naval gunfire

stacking.title: Stacking Limits
notes: A Bn. unit is always a Bn. unit regardless of remaining steps (6.0).
Players must meet stacking limits at the end of each Movement/Exploit Phase. Exceptions: Strat mode (5.5.5), Mech road movement (7.10.3), entering PA mode (5.2.1).
Special stacking (6.2.0): Co. must be a one-step Co. or a two-step Co. already reduced to one step.
Units stacked using special stacking are treated as one unit for stacking, moving, and Max step loss purposes.
Overstacked units suffer a one-column unfavorable GA shift and cannot enter PA/Strat mode or use Mech road movement (6.7.0).

baseLimits.title: Base Stacking Limits (6.1.0)
rows: May substitute Co.'s for Bn. units (e.g., 3 Co.'s instead of 2 Bn. + 1 Co.)
May substitute Co.'s for Bn. units
footnotes: Plus any units qualifying under special stacking (6.2.0).
Z-step units have no effect on stacking other than their own limits.

specialStacking.title: Special Stacking — Maximum Units by Nationality

sections.title: German — Non-Constricted (6.3.0)
rows: Any Bn. (if Mech class: +1 AFV Co.) and 1 Eng Co. or 1 AT/TD Co.
Any Bn. (if Mech class: +1 AFV Co.) and 1 Eng Co. or 1 AT/TD Co.
Any non-Bn. unit (if Mech class: +1 AFV Co.) or if hybrid Co.: +1 Eng Co. or AT/TD Co.
sections.maxUnits: Max 10 units (if all Mech Bn. with AFV Co.); 7 without free-stacked Co.'s

sections.title: German — Constricted (6.4.0)
rows: Any Bn. (if Mech class: +1 AFV Co.) and 1 Eng Co. or 1 AT/TD Co.
Any non-Bn. unit (if Mech class: +1 AFV Co.) or if hybrid Co.: +1 Eng Co. or AT/TD Co.
Any non-Bn. unit (if Mech class) +1 AFV Co.

sections.title: Allied — Non-Constricted (6.3.0 / 6.2.2)
rows: Any Bn. +1 AT/TD or Eng Co.
Any Bn. +1 AT/TD or Eng Co.
Any non-Bn. unit +1 AT/TD or Eng Co.
sections.maxUnits: Max 5 units (plus free-stacked Co.'s; max 2 free Eng/AT Co.'s in non-constricted)

sections.title: Allied — Constricted (6.4.0 / 6.2.2)
rows: Any Bn. +1 Eng Co. or 1 AT/TD Co.
Any non-Bn. unit +1 Eng Co. or 1 AT/TD Co.
sections.maxUnits: Max 4 units (max 1 free Eng or AT Co. in constricted)

freeStackingLimits.title: Free-Stacked Unit Limits
rows: One-step AFV Co. stacks free with Mech class personnel/Recon Co. or Bn. of same formation
One-step Eng or AT Co. stacks free with Bn.-sized personnel/Recon unit or two-step hybrid unit
Substitutes for permitted Eng/AT Co. with US Arm Cav Bn. (not in addition to)

mechRoadStacking.title: Mech Road Movement Stacking (7.10.3)
mechRoadStacking.description: The number of Mech class units in a hex may restrict Mech road movement. Units using special stacking (6.2.0) or two one-step Mech Co.'s moving together do not count.
rules: May enter a hex with 1 Mech unit by paying +2 MP; must stop in that hex.
May enter a hex with >1 Mech unit only if special stacking (6.2.0) results in ≤2 Mech units at phase end.
Cannot enter a hex with >1 Mech unit if special stacking cannot reduce to 2.

mechUnitsAsLeg.title: Mech Units Treated as Leg for Stacking (6.5.0)
mechUnitsAsLeg.description: When determining Strat mode and Mech road movement stacking, treat these Mech class units as Leg class:
units: Mech class Eng units conducting any form of construction
